docs.title: John's 2026 ROOK MK2
docs.text:
Welcome...

These are my modificaitions of Rolohauns Rook MK2 3D printer!. If you want the official version visit that link.

docs.title: Specifications
docs.text:
• 120 x120mm Build Area
  • 4:1 Gear Reduction Z
  • Cartesian Motion for simplicity
  • Fully 3D printed Frame
  • Dragonburner Toolhead with wide extruder and hotend support
  • Sensorless and Endstop homing support

This is my documentation. There are many like it, but this one is mine.

My documentation is my best friend. It is my life. I must master it as I must master my printer.

Without my printer, my documentation is useless. Without my documentation, my printer is useless. I must explain every part clearly. I must illustrate better than the confusion that threatens to jam my extruder. I must guide the build before frustration sets in.

I will show every step. I will label every screw, rod, and pulley. I will document every wire, sensor, and motor. I will include images, notes, and tips that prevent mistakes.

I am the master of my documentation. I am the guardian of clarity. I am the protector of the build.

My documentation, with me, is powerful. It will instruct. It will prevent errors. It will save hours of trial and frustration. I will keep it up to date. I will keep it precise. I will keep it accessible.

I will never leave a step ambiguous. I will never skip a warning. I will always maintain it.

This is my documentation. There are many like it, but this one is mine.

docs.title: Warning
docs.text:
Early Release

This is Version 0 of this manual. The printer is not finished and neither is this.

docs.title: 2. Attach rail and sides to gantry
Add pin pulley and motors to gantry
Add the rest of the pins and pulleys
Add the top of the frame
mount bed holder to rails (also attach endstop bar)
insert springs and mount bed to holder
Belts
docs.text:
The Rook MK2 uses Cartesian motion with belts and apex clips to move the print head and build platform. Proper belt installation is essential for accurate and smooth motion. All belts attach using apex clips, which lock the belt in place without requiring screws.

Note

This guide applies to all axes (Both X, Y, and Z). The belt installation method is the same for each.

  1. Route the belt around the motor pulley, idler, and guide as shown in the frame diagram.
  2. Attach the belt using apex clip to one side:
    • Insert the belt end fully into the provide hole, making sure the teeth align properly to the hole.
    • Insert the apex clip on the oposite side.
    • Press firmly until the clip clicks into place.
  3. Route the belt through the pulley tensioner and to the other side:
    • Make sure tensioner pulley if fully extended so you can later pull the tension tight.
    • Insert belt fully into the opposite hole making sure the teeth are oriented correctly.
    • Finally insert the apex clip securing the belt.
    • This allows you to adjust the belt tension later after fully assembled.
  4. Check basic movement:
    • Move the axis manually to ensure the belt runs smoothly and does not slip.
  5. Adjust tension once assembly is complete:
    • Use the screw to adjust the slide tensioner and fine-tune the belt tension until there is no slack but the motion remains smooth.

Tip

  • Align belt teeth carefully with pulleys to prevent skipping.
  • Avoid over-tightening; excessive tension can damage motors or pulleys.
  • Double-check all axes for smooth, free movement before final calibration.

docs.title: BOM
docs.text:
Go through this list and cross off anything you have. Then add up what is left is what your printer will cost.

Things that are not listed here are 3 partial spools of filament, your time, patience and last remaining hairs.

The purchasing suggestions here will have leftover parts to start your next project.

You may find some parts cheaper on Aliexpress if you want to wait a month.

docs.title: Shopping list
docs.text: Qty Hardware 88 Heatsets M3x5x4 $10 1 1600pcs M3 Screw Kit $18 1 443pcs M3 Long Screw Kit $10 3 MGN9C 200mm for Y and X $30.00 2 MGN9C 150mm for Z $20.00 4 F695 Bearing Pack of 10 $7.00 3 Nema17 42mm Any you prefer for Y and Z $11.00 1 Nema17 Pancake Stepper 23mm MAX Body Length for X $10.00 GT2 6mm wide Belt 6 meters $9.00 5 20 Tooth 5mm Bore Pulley $9.00 4 20 Tooth 5mm Bore Idler $10.00 1 80 Tooth Pulley for Z $10.00 1 188mm GT2 Belt Loop for Z (7mm tall with t) $15.00 2 5mm to 5mm bore coupler (Inner Diameter: 5mm-5mm(D14xL25)) $7.00 3 400mm CF or Steel Rod 5mm Diameter $10.00 4 Rubber Feet or print TPU SUB TOTAL $148.00 v0 BED Spring Steel & Magnet 120x120mm $15.00 Aluminum v0 Bed $20.00 v0 Heater $15.00 Bed Springs and M3 Bolts $4.00 Extruder + Hotend (Dragonburner) Bambu X1 Clone hotend $13.00 SHerpa Extruder $30.00 PC4-M10 Pneumatic Fittings PTFE Tube Qty Electronics 1 24 Volt 6 Amp Power Supply AC Adapter $24.00 1 DC Power Jack Socket, 5.5mm x 2.5mm Female Barrel Panel Mount $7 optional Optical Endstop ( If not using Biqu Micro Probe ) $1.00 2 or 3 Endstop Omron ( If not using sensorless homeing ) $6.00 optional Microprobe $27.00 urchoice BIGTREETECH DZ-01 MCU $85.00 1 Spool of wiring $18.00 1 630PCS JST PH Kit PH2.0mm Connectors Set $10.00 1 3010 24v ball bearing fan for hotend $11.00 2 4010 24vball bearing fan for layer cooling $11.00

docs.title: Part Prep
docs.text:
Cut the 5mm rod into these lengths:

  • Rod 1: 220mm + 60mm + 3x 40mm
  • Rod 2: 220mm + 3x 40mm + 2x 30mm
  • Rod 3: 5x40mm + 2x15mm

This will give you the following:

qty Rod Cut List purpose 2 220mm X Y motors 1 60mm Pin for Z 2 15mm Pin for Y Idlers 1 30mm Pin for X Idler 11 40mm Pin for frame

Cut the belt into these lengths:

Qty Belt Cut List purpose 1  mm Z 2 mm X 1  mm Y

These are the screws that you will need:

Qty Screw purpose 8 M3 x 6mm 29 M3 x 8mm 42 M3 x 10mm 9 M3 x 12mm 11 M3 x 16mm 4 M3 x 20mm 4 M3 x 25mm 13 M3 x 35mm 4 M5 x 30mm Qty JST purpose 4 6 pin female 4 wire to stepper motor  4 4 pin female 4 wire to mcu for stepper 3 2 pin female 2 wire to mcu for endstop

docs.title: Introduction
docs.text:
Warning

Assembling a 3D printer is a time consuming and dangerous process.

If you have not built anything before this project can kill you and may seriously hurt you.

This machine can maim, burn and electrocute you if you are not careful.

We take ZERO responsibility for your ability or education, this information is for educational purposes only.

Please, read and reread the entire manual fully and carefully before you start assembly.

NOTE: This is an unfinished beta machine so it may not even work as described.

docs.title: Print Settings
docs.text:
MaterialABS, ASAInfill typeLocked zag, gyroid, cubicInfill Percent40%Layer Height0.2mmWall Count4Extrusion Width0.4mmSolid Top/Bottom Layers5

Note: This printer was designed so it could be printed with PLA and no heated bed, although I have not tested this. YMMV

docs.title: Printing
docs.text: When you layout the parts I suggest printing only one part at a time. That way if it the room is too cold and the parts curl up you are not wasting a lot of filament.
